caption: 2017 financial framework: data mid-to-high-single-digit growth, low-single-digit expense growth, double-digit earnings growth and FCF-funded returns.
Acquisition hurdle card: greater-than-10% target returns, ROIC above cost of capital, 90%+ synergies in three years, and moat/IP/wallet-share tests.
Product roadmap from M&A: acquired assets recombined into ICE Index, data evaluations, bond, CDS, Connect and private-cloud offerings.
2017 data TAM: ICE framed pricing, reference, valuation, real-time, trading and exchange data as differentiated growth pools.
Old energy proof point: ICE energy revenue and open interest compounded across cycles, anchored by Brent and gas liquidity.
Clearing leadership evidence: product breadth, daily cleared contract volume and clearing-house financial resources.
NYSE competitive framing versus Nasdaq: market quality, listings, ETF and trading leadership claims.
Management moat framing in 2017: being early to OTC automation, clearing, data-as-an-asset, exchange scarcity and mortgage digitization.
Original mortgage thesis: fragmented paper closings, MERS/eNotes and ICE data/connectivity as the route to a digital mortgage network.
Post-Ellie Mae mortgage platform slide: workflow coverage from lead generation through post-closing, with roughly $10B TAM versus $1.2B ICE revenue.
Pre-Black Knight strategy bridge: secular tailwinds across energy, FIDS and mortgage, including 85% U.S. mortgage connectivity.
Black Knight/Optimal Blue deal math: divestiture price and intact $200M cost plus $125M net revenue synergy targets.
Energy proof point: 1H20-1H25 revenue CAGR and mix across Brent, other oil, global natural gas and environmentals.
FIDS proof point: ASV growth plus ICE Bonds and CDS Clearing revenue CAGRs from 1H20 to 1H25.
Mortgage platform workflow: ICE positions data, technology and network expertise across six loan-lifecycle jobs.
Twenty-five-year innovation timeline linking energy, clearing, data, fixed income, mortgage, climate data and Polymarket.
AI roadmap across ICE: energy demand, proprietary datasets, product velocity, network capacity and mortgage automation.
Current long-term model: balanced recurring/transaction revenue, 62% adjusted operating margin, about 49% recurring revenue and about $4.5B adjusted FCF.
Current energy map and mix: global crude, gas, LNG and environmental benchmarks with roughly $2.4B TTM energy revenue.
FIDS KPI slide: subscription ASV growth and roughly 81% recurring segment revenue.
Mortgage segment economics: revenue mix by origination, closing, servicing and data/analytics, with recurring versus transaction split.
Capital return framework and history: dividends, buybacks, M&A pauses and the stated excess-FCF repurchase rule.
Current M&A framework repeats the 2017 acquisition hurdles and strategic-benefit requirements.
FIDS product map and revenue models across pricing/reference data, network technology, ICE Bonds and CDS clearing.
Mortgage product map and revenue models from Encompass and network services to MERS, MSP, DDA and Analyzer.
Five-year financial proof points: net revenue, adjusted operating income, EPS, operating cash flow and adjusted FCF CAGRs.
Current FY2026 guidance: recurring growth targets, mortgage growth target, expense range, capex/software and share count.
